name: AI Data Pipeline
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: GPT-4o-mini for data classification, entity extraction, enrichment, and schema inference

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput for high-volume batch processing

services.name: Azure Data Factory
services.purpose: Pipeline orchestration — data extraction, transformation scheduling, LLM-enrichment stages

enterprise.note: ~100K activity runs/month, self-hosted IR, complex data flows

services.name: Azure Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Enriched data store — classified records, entity graphs, pipeline metadata

services.name: Azure Event Hubs
services.purpose: Real-time data ingestion — streaming records into classification pipeline

services.name: Azure Functions
services.purpose: Event-triggered processors — record classification, entity extraction, enrichment workers

enterprise.note: Always-warm, VNet, high memory for batch LLM calls

services.name: Blob Storage
services.purpose: Raw data landing zone — CSV, JSON, Parquet files before and after enrichment

prod.note: ~1TB, lifecycle policies to Cool after 30 days

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: OpenAI API keys, Data Factory connection secrets, Event Hubs SAS keys

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Pipeline latency, classification accuracy, token consumption, enrichment throughput

services.name: Log Analytics
services.purpose: Centralized logging — pipeline run history, classification errors, Data Factory diagnostics

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for classification tasks — 15x cheaper than GPT-4o with comparable accuracy for structured extraction
Batch records in groups of 20-50 per LLM call — amortizes overhead and reduces total API calls by 90%
Implement a classification cache — identical record patterns reuse prior LLM results, saving 30-40% token cost
Use Data Factory mapping data flows only for complex transforms — activity-level orchestration is significantly cheaper
Move enriched data to Cool storage after 30 days — 60% savings on storage for historical records
Stream low-priority records through Event Hubs Basic tier — reserve Standard for production workloads
